title: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) Advances Toward 2026 Spaceflight Goals
text: Virgin Galactic continues to advance toward its 2026 commercial spaceflight goals. CEO Michael Colglazier reported strong progress advancing the build of new SpaceShips in Q1 2026, keeping pace with plans to begin commercial spaceflight in 2026. The company demonstrated momentum with the successful transfer of its first new spaceship from the assembly hangar to the test-and-launch facility. Ground testing is underway, and the company remains on track to commence flight testing in Q3 and spaceflight operations in Q4 of 2026. The company has begun accepting bookings for spaceflights at $750,000 per seat, with approximately 650 reservations already made. Despite this progress, Virgin Galactic faces significant financial headwinds. Management noted that the company's cash outlook raises substantial doubt about whether operations can last another 12 months. The transfer of the first Delta-class spaceship to the test facility represents a critical milestone for Virgin Galactic's path toward returning to commercial spaceflight operations.
